description: Create a physics-based simulation of a basketball bank shot in GeoGebra to visualize the trajectory of a ball reflecting off a backboard. The simulation should use gravity g = 9.8 m/s², a release height of (0, 2) meters, a vertical backboard at x = 4.6 meters, and a hoop at y = 3.05 meters (rim center at x = 4.15). Include sliders for initial velocity (v0, range 5 to 15 m/s), angle (theta, range 30° to 85°), and coefficient of restitution (e, range 0 to 1). The trajectory must model standard projectile motion until it hits the backboard, followed by an elastic collision rebound where the horizontal velocity reverses and scales by the coefficient of restitution (vx_new = -e * vx_old) while vertical motion continues under gravity. Visualize the flight path and the backboard segment. Save the simulation to ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/bank_shot.ggb.
